behaviors.description: A file placed inside a subdirectory of the watched root is processed, not only files placed directly in the root directory.
behaviors.justification: The failure mode depends on real filesystem watcher behavior for nested paths (OS event delivery + watcher recursion semantics), and success is an externally visible side effect (file is actually processed). Unit-only tests of path filters can pass while real watch wiring misses subdirectory events. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no AI/ML decision is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Start the daemon against a real temporary watched directory on disk, create a subdirectory, write a file into that subdirectory, and assert via round-trip observation (e.g., output artifact/logged completion) that exactly that file was processed.

behaviors.description: The deduplication logic correctly discards a second processing request for the same file path within a configurable time window.
behaviors.justification: This is primarily local-module handoff correctness (request intake + dedup state + pipeline dispatch) and does not explicitly require real OS/network/subprocess contracts. Unit tests for a pure dedup helper can miss wiring/state propagation bugs between modules, so Integration is the lowest additional reality check. Rejected higher level: System is disqualified by behavior text because it does not require validating real external response/exit-code contracts or OS-specific behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the real intake, dedup store, and dispatcher with a controllable clock; submit two requests for the same path within the configured window and assert only one dispatch occurs, then submit after the window and assert dispatch resumes.

behaviors.description: When the operating system emits multiple filesystem events for a single file write, the daemon processes the file exactly once.
behaviors.justification: The behavior explicitly depends on OS filesystem event patterns, which mocks often hide. The test must verify externally visible once-only processing under real event duplication conditions, satisfying System-level reality checks. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model output is part of correctness.
behaviors.test_description: Run the daemon against a real watched directory; perform a single write pattern known to trigger multiple events (or explicitly emit rapid successive real writes/renames) and assert only one processing outcome is produced for that file.

behaviors.description: If the watched directory is removed and then recreated while the daemon is running, the daemon resumes watching the new directory without a restart.
behaviors.justification: Directory deletion/recreation while live is an OS/filesystem lifecycle condition; in-process fakes can miss watcher re-subscription failures. Success is an externally visible round-trip effect: after recreate, a new file is actually detected and processed without restart. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because there is no AI/ML dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Start daemon on a real directory, remove that directory, recreate it at the same path, then write a new file and assert it is processed without restarting the daemon process.

behaviors.description: When multiple files arrive at the same time, they are processed in the order determined by their creation timestamps.
behaviors.justification: Ordering by filesystem creation timestamps is sensitive to real filesystem metadata resolution and event timing; unit/integration tests with synthetic timestamps can give false confidence. The behavior is only proven when real arrivals and resulting processing order are observed end-to-end. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because ordering is not based on AI decisions.
behaviors.test_description: Using a real watched directory, create multiple files in a tight time window, capture the daemon’s processed order, and assert it matches the files’ real creation-time ordering as read from the filesystem metadata policy used by the daemon.

behaviors.description: A file that is still being written to when the arrival event fires is not picked up for processing until writing is complete.
behaviors.justification: This race condition depends on real filesystem write visibility and watcher timing; mocks often cannot reproduce partial-write/readiness behavior faithfully. Correctness requires round-trip observation that processing starts only after completion of real writes. Rejected higher levels: Agentic/Workflow are disqualified because no model judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: With the daemon running on a real directory, begin a slow/streamed write to a file (keep handle open), confirm processing has not occurred mid-write, then close the writer and assert processing occurs afterward exactly once.
